panels.A: Bounded-variance compliance versus drift for Proposed, GELU, and SELU under h1 regimes.
panels.B: Forgetting index versus drift for the same methods with 95% confidence intervals across seeds.
panels.C: Regret-slope scaling with conflict margin at short switch periods for h4 stress regimes.
panels.D: Forgetting-floor trend versus conflict margin, contrasting static and stateful methods.

variables.bounded_variance_compliance: Fraction of runs satisfying variance-bound criterion.
variables.regret_slope: Estimated slope of dynamic regret over stream length (loss/step).

key_takeaways: Proposed full model stays near the >=0.95 compliance threshold in bounded drift and degrades under stress drift as theory predicts.
Static baselines show steeper regret-slope increase in high-conflict regimes than the proposed stateful method.
paper/figures/validation_panels.pdf.uncertainty: Line bands are 95% confidence intervals over seed-level runs.

panels.A: Cross-hypothesis ablation impact on bounded-variance compliance.
panels.B: Cross-hypothesis ablation impact on forgetting index.

key_takeaways: Removing the slow anchor produces the largest stability degradation.
Static GELU has the weakest forgetting behavior in the ablation suite.
paper/figures/ablation_panels.pdf.uncertainty: Bar whiskers show 95% confidence intervals over seeded runs.

acceptance_snapshot.description: Bounded drift compliance >= 0.95 for proposed method
Stress drift should reduce compliance vs bounded regime
Static baselines have positive regret slope under high conflict
Dynamic model slope reduction >= 30% vs best static
Boundary case weakens slope vs high conflict

confirmatory_analysis.name: Regime-stratified post-selection effect-size check
confirmatory_analysis.notes: Computed Cohen's d for forgetting improvement of selected top bounded-regime method vs GELU in bounded and stress regimes.
